headline: Congresswoman Discloses Hundreds Of Thousands In Stock Trades, Here's What She Bought
summary: Stock trading by members of Congress continues to draw increased attention from retail traders. A recent disclosure shows a member of Congress who made no stock transactions in 2025 is now back buying stocks again. Congresswoman Goes Stock Shopping in 2026 Congresswoman Maria Elvira Salazar (R-Fla.) disclosed multiple stock purchases made during March with no sales, according to the Benzinga Government Trades page. Here's the list of stocks bought in the latest disclosure: March 25: FedEx Corp.,

headline: Amgen’s AI-Focused Leadership Reshuffle Could Be A Game Changer For Amgen (AMGN)
summary: Amgen recently announced that David M. Reese, M.D., its long-serving executive vice president and Chief Technology Officer, will retire on June 30, 2026, alongside a broad leadership reshuffle emphasizing research, artificial intelligence, data, technology, and global markets. The creation of a dedicated executive role for Research and Development, Artificial Intelligence and Data signals a company-wide push to fuse advanced analytics with drug discovery, development, and...
